title: Model of Simplicial Complexes with dimension-wise preferential attachment
authors: Diego Febbe, Duccio Fanelli, Timoteo Carletti
abstract: Network science is a powerful framework allowing to model complex systems, it is capable to describe and take into account the intricate web of connections existing among the constituting basic element of the system. Recently scholars have brought to the fore the relevance of higher-order networks, namely structures allowing to encode for many-body interaction, differently from the pairwise case handled by networks. This novel research field opens new avenues of research with applications ranging from neurosciences to social sciences; there is thus a need for generative models of higher-order network capable to reproduce features present in empirical data. In this work we present a model for growing simplicial complex rooted on a preferential attachment process acting dimension-wise, i.e., returning a power law distribution for the generalized degree of simplexes of different dimension.
summary: This paper introduces a new generative model for growing simplicial complexes using a preferential attachment mechanism that acts on different dimensions. It demonstrates how the model can produce power-law degree distributions and reveals how the interplay between different dimensional components can trigger abrupt transitions in the network's scaling properties.
main_problem: The need for generative models of higher-order networks (simplicial complexes) that can reproduce the topological and spectral features found in empirical many-body interaction data.
main_result: The authors develop a dimension-wise preferential attachment model that produces power-law degree distributions and identify an abrupt transition in scaling exponents when moving between different dimensional attachment probabilities.
method: The study uses algebraic topology (Hodge-Laplacian, boundary operators), continuous differential equation approximations for growth dynamics, and spectral analysis of the $d$-Laplacian.
model_or_system: A growing simplicial complex where new nodes and higher-dimensional simplices (links, triangles, tetrahedra) are added at each time step based on dimension-specific attachment probabilities $p_d$.
key_observables: Generalized degree distribution, spectral dimension ($d_s$), Betti numbers ($ eta_d$), Fiedler eigenvalue, and scaling exponents ($ eta, \gamma$).
important_parameters: Maximum dimension $D$, attachment probabilities $p_1, \dots, p_D$, and a flavor parameter $s$.
main_assumptions: The growth process assumes a specific probabilistic attachment rule where the probability of selecting a simplex is proportional to its generalized degree.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the basic building blocks (simplices) and their assembly; Figure 2 illustrates the growth process; Figure 4 shows the transition of the growth exponent $ eta_0$ as a function of $p_1$; Figure 6 and 7 visualize the hierarchical construction and the relationship between node and link degrees.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the higher-order network framework, introduces the dimension-wise preferential attachment growth algorithm, provides analytical derivations for degree evolution and scaling exponents, explores specific dimensional regimes and phase transitions, and concludes with numerical validation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
